title: Ashwagandha has the clearest anxiety fit when the pattern is anticipatory or ongoing worry rather than acute event-linked nervousness.
claim: Ashwagandha has the clearest anxiety fit when the pattern is anticipatory or ongoing worry rather than acute event-linked nervousness.

title: Ashwagandha has the clearest stress fit when the issue is ongoing perceived stress rather than a single acute spike.
claim: Ashwagandha has the clearest stress fit when the issue is ongoing perceived stress rather than a single acute spike.

title: Bacopa monnieri has the most consistent human trial evidence among botanicals for sustained attention and information processing speed.
claim: Bacopa monnieri has the most consistent human trial evidence among botanicals for sustained attention and information processing speed.

title: Bacopa may reduce working memory load and improve cognitive performance under conditions of mental complexity.
claim: Bacopa may reduce working memory load and improve cognitive performance under conditions of mental complexity.

title: Ginkgo biloba has moderate human evidence for supporting attention speed and accuracy, particularly in populations with attention-related concerns.
claim: Ginkgo biloba has moderate human evidence for supporting attention speed and accuracy, particularly in populations with attention-related concerns.

title: Glycine may be worth researching when the goal is subjective sleep quality without a strongly sedating approach.
claim: Glycine may be worth researching when the goal is subjective sleep quality without a strongly sedating approach.

title: Kava has the strongest acute anxiolytic evidence among the botanical options but carries a liver safety signal that changes the risk calculus.
claim: Kava has the strongest acute anxiolytic evidence among the botanical options but carries a liver safety signal that changes the risk calculus.

title: L-theanine is a plausible first-pass option when the stress pattern is acute tension or task-linked physiological arousal.
claim: L-theanine is a plausible first-pass option when the stress pattern is acute tension or task-linked physiological arousal.

title: L-theanine may support sustained attention and reduce mind-wandering without producing sedation.
claim: L-theanine may support sustained attention and reduce mind-wandering without producing sedation.

title: L-theanine is a plausible first-pass option when the barrier to sleep is mental tension rather than circadian timing.
claim: L-theanine is a plausible first-pass option when the barrier to sleep is mental tension rather than circadian timing.

title: L-theanine may reduce cognitive over-activity or worry-loop patterns associated with racing thoughts.
claim: L-theanine may reduce cognitive over-activity or worry-loop patterns associated with racing thoughts.

title: L-theanine is a plausible first-pass option when anxiety is situational, acute, or tied to performance pressure.
claim: L-theanine is a plausible first-pass option when anxiety is situational, acute, or tied to performance pressure.

title: L-theanine may be a gentle fit when stress carries into sleep quality or bedtime cognitive tension.
claim: L-theanine may be a gentle fit when stress carries into sleep quality or bedtime cognitive tension.

title: L-theanine combined with caffeine has the strongest acute evidence for reducing task-switching errors and attention carryover.
claim: L-theanine combined with caffeine has the strongest acute evidence for reducing task-switching errors and attention carryover.

title: Lemon balm is best framed as a gentle relaxation candidate rather than a high-certainty sleep intervention.
claim: Lemon balm is best framed as a gentle relaxation candidate rather than a high-certainty sleep intervention.

title: Lemon balm is a mild first-step option for situational nervousness where a strong sedating effect is not desired.
claim: Lemon balm is a mild first-step option for situational nervousness where a strong sedating effect is not desired.

title: Lion's mane mushroom has early-stage human evidence suggesting benefits for cognitive function that may be relevant to cognitive load capacity.
claim: Lion's mane mushroom has early-stage human evidence suggesting benefits for cognitive function that may be relevant to cognitive load capacity.

title: Magnesium is most decision-useful when stress overlaps with low intake context, muscle tension, or low-magnesium risk signals.
claim: Magnesium is most decision-useful when stress overlaps with low intake context, muscle tension, or low-magnesium risk signals.

title: Magnesium is most decision-useful for anxiety when somatic tension, muscle tightness, or low-intake signals are part of the presentation.
claim: Magnesium is most decision-useful for anxiety when somatic tension, muscle tightness, or low-intake signals are part of the presentation.

title: Magnesium may be most decision-useful when poor sleep overlaps with low intake context, tension, or older-adult sleep quality concerns.
claim: Magnesium may be most decision-useful when poor sleep overlaps with low intake context, tension, or older-adult sleep quality concerns.

title: Melatonin has the clearest fit when the sleep problem is circadian timing or difficulty falling asleep.
claim: Melatonin has the clearest fit when the sleep problem is circadian timing or difficulty falling asleep.

title: Passionflower is a plausible option when racing thoughts or cognitive restlessness dominate the anxiety pattern.
claim: Passionflower is a plausible option when racing thoughts or cognitive restlessness dominate the anxiety pattern.

title: Phosphatidylserine is a niche option for stress-reactivity questions involving cortisol or ACTH response patterns.
claim: Phosphatidylserine is a niche option for stress-reactivity questions involving cortisol or ACTH response patterns.

title: PK/bioavailability confirmed; food effect increases absorption; 5-LOX/inflammation.
claim: PK/bioavailability confirmed; food effect increases absorption; 5-LOX/inflammation.

title: Phase I PK (black cohosh); t½ ~2h, no acute toxicity.
claim: Phase I PK (black cohosh); t½ ~2h, no acute toxicity.

title: Immunomodulatory/wound-healing (preclinical dominant).
claim: Immunomodulatory/wound-healing (preclinical dominant).

title: Anti-inflammatory (NF-κB/5-LOX); extract OA trials.
claim: Anti-inflammatory (NF-κB/5-LOX); extract OA trials.

title: Linked to leukotriene suppression in Boswellia; preclinical dominant.
claim: Linked to leukotriene suppression in Boswellia; preclinical dominant.

title: Venotonic for CVI (strong Cochrane-level extract evidence).
claim: Venotonic for CVI (strong Cochrane-level extract evidence).

title: Variable bioavailability; cholesterol/antimicrobial in garlic preparations.
claim: Variable bioavailability; cholesterol/antimicrobial in garlic preparations.

title: CNS/cholinergic; preclinical anxiolytic.
claim: CNS/cholinergic; preclinical anxiolytic.

title: Inflammatory/apoptotic; rat PK.
claim: Inflammatory/apoptotic; rat PK.

claim: Nicotinic receptor activity; limited direct human data; primarily mechanistic/toxicology context.

claim: Inflammatory pathway effects; nicotinic signaling; preclinical dominant.

claim: Cardioprotective triterpenoid; preclinical signaling data.

title: Strong drug evidence: Human RCT evidence supports improved glycemic control in type 2 diabetes; UKPDS 44 randomized 1,946 participants and found lower HbA1c with acarbose vs placebo over 3 years, with GI side effects affecting adherence.
claim: Strong drug evidence: Human RCT evidence supports improved glycemic control in type 2 diabetes; UKPDS 44 randomized 1,946 participants and found lower HbA1c with acarbose vs placebo over 3 years, with GI side effects affecting adherence.

title: Limited human evidence: Human RCT evidence exists in metabolic syndrome: 24 participants received Banaba 500 mg twice daily or placebo for 12 weeks; trial evaluated MetS components, insulin sensitivity, and insulin secretion.
claim: Limited human evidence: Human RCT evidence exists in metabolic syndrome: 24 participants received Banaba 500 mg twice daily or placebo for 12 weeks; trial evaluated MetS components, insulin sensitivity, and insulin secretion.

title: Limited-to-moderate human evidence: Clinical evidence review identified randomized placebo-controlled trials in osteoarthritis/back pain; higher-quality studies suggested pain reduction, but trial quality limitations prevent strong certainty.
claim: Limited-to-moderate human evidence: Clinical evidence review identified randomized placebo-controlled trials in osteoarthritis/back pain; higher-quality studies suggested pain reduction, but trial quality limitations prevent strong certainty.

title: Moderate prebiotic evidence: Human systematic review/meta-analysis evidence supports fructooligosaccharides as a prebiotic affecting gut microbiota composition; strongest signal is microbiome modulation rather than direct disease treatment.
claim: Moderate prebiotic evidence: Human systematic review/meta-analysis evidence supports fructooligosaccharides as a prebiotic affecting gut microbiota composition; strongest signal is microbiome modulation rather than direct disease treatment.

title: Limited human evidence: Human randomized double-blind placebo-controlled trial evidence exists in 28 patients with metabolic syndrome using 12 mg/day for 12 weeks; endpoint-specific extraction should be reviewed before strong claims.

title: Low/mixed human evidence: Human RCT evidence is mixed/negative. A 12-week JAMA randomized placebo-controlled trial in 135 overweight adults found no significant weight or fat-mass loss beyond placebo; other small trials/reviews are inconsistent.
claim: Low/mixed human evidence: Human RCT evidence is mixed/negative. A 12-week JAMA randomized placebo-controlled trial in 135 overweight adults found no significant weight or fat-mass loss beyond placebo; other small trials/reviews are inconsistent.

title: Limited-to-moderate human evidence: Human clinical evidence for magnesium bisglycinate/glycinate is emerging; a randomized placebo-controlled trial in adults with poor sleep tested magnesium bisglycinate, while broader magnesium evidence supports sleep/BP contexts depending on population and deficiency.
claim: Limited-to-moderate human evidence: Human clinical evidence for magnesium bisglycinate/glycinate is emerging; a randomized placebo-controlled trial in adults with poor sleep tested magnesium bisglycinate, while broader magnesium evidence supports sleep/BP contexts depending on population and deficiency.

title: Mixed human evidence: Human evidence is mixed. Cochrane review found adjunctive benefits in chronic heart failure trials, but the later HERB CHF randomized trial found no symptomatic/functional benefit when added to standard therapy.
claim: Mixed human evidence: Human evidence is mixed. Cochrane review found adjunctive benefits in chronic heart failure trials, but the later HERB CHF randomized trial found no symptomatic/functional benefit when added to standard therapy.

title: Moderate human evidence: Human evidence supports Iberogast/STW 5 in functional dyspepsia symptom reduction based on systematic review evidence and more recent randomized trials evaluating gastric gas tolerance.
claim: Moderate human evidence: Human evidence supports Iberogast/STW 5 in functional dyspepsia symptom reduction based on systematic review evidence and more recent randomized trials evaluating gastric gas tolerance.

title: Moderate-to-strong human evidence: Human randomized trial evidence supports oral lavender oil preparation Silexan for anxiety symptoms, including GAD trials vs placebo and comparator arms.
claim: Moderate-to-strong human evidence: Human randomized trial evidence supports oral lavender oil preparation Silexan for anxiety symptoms, including GAD trials vs placebo and comparator arms.

title: Limited-to-moderate human evidence: Human evidence includes randomized trials and a systematic review suggesting Passionflower may reduce anxiety in some contexts; sleep evidence includes a placebo-controlled polysomnography insomnia study.

title: Moderate human evidence: Human RCT and meta-analysis evidence supports saffron for mood-related outcomes. Affron 28 mg/day improved mood/stress/anxiety in low-mood adults over 4 weeks; MDD meta-analysis evidence exists but claims should remain condition-specific.
claim: Moderate human evidence: Human RCT and meta-analysis evidence supports saffron for mood-related outcomes. Affron 28 mg/day improved mood/stress/anxiety in low-mood adults over 4 weeks; MDD meta-analysis evidence exists but claims should remain condition-specific.

title: Moderate human evidence: Human randomized controlled trial evidence supports native/undenatured type II collagen for knee osteoarthritis-related symptoms and function, with newer RCT literature continuing to evaluate UC-II/hydrolyzed collagen combinations.
claim: Moderate human evidence: Human randomized controlled trial evidence supports native/undenatured type II collagen for knee osteoarthritis-related symptoms and function, with newer RCT literature continuing to evaluate UC-II/hydrolyzed collagen combinations.
